output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can legally deduct, and how to demand your money back today.
content_outline: Introduction: The Frustration of a Withheld Security Deposit
Is My Landlord Required to Return My Deposit? Know the Legal Deadlines
What Can a Landlord Deduct from Your Security Deposit? (And What They Can't)
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? Understanding Wrongful Withholding
What to Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit: A Step-by-Step Guide
How to Write an Effective Security Deposit Dispute Letter
When to Escalate: Small Claims Court and Beyond
Conclusion: Take Action Before Time Runs Out
key_points: Landlords must return security deposits within a strict state-specific timeframe, typically between 14 and 45 days
Normal wear and tear, minor scuffs, and routine cleaning cannot be legally deducted from your deposit
A professionally written demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover your money without immediately going to court
DepositBack generates state-compliant security deposit demand letters in minutes, applying legal pressure so landlords pay attention
output.cta: Stop waiting and start recovering. Create your state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in under 10 minutes—no lawyer needed.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need immediate, actionable guidance on tenant rights and recovery steps
